candidate_summary: Adaptive null-space LoRA adapter with learned slack parameter sigma controlling the WIDTH of the null-space tube around pretrained W; constraint ||P_null_perp(dW)||_F <= sigma is relaxed/contracted per-layer based on data distribution drift (open/closed pores analog from Bedouin black tent goat-hair weave). Riemannian Stiefel projector restores dW into the sigma-tube each gradient step (rope-restake analog). Combined with LoRA-Subtraction Drift-Resistant-Space objective.
content_words: coarse weave loose dry heat convection
fibers swell rain pores close watertight

results.name: Riemannian Optimization for LoRA on the Stiefel Manifold (2508.17901)
results.description: Stiefel manifold optimization for LoRA's B matrix using retraction + vector transport
keyword_matches: LoRA (adaptive slack-tension null-space LoRA / orthogonal subspace adapter)
results.rationale: EXACT TWIN on the Stiefel rope-restore mechanism — Riemannian retraction on Stiefel manifold for LoRA B factor IS the Stiefel rope-restore projection per-step. Lacks the adaptive sigma-tube width relaxation aspect (strict Stiefel rather than slack-tube), so not perfect on the full mechanism, but covers the headline novel sub-component. Functional same-artefact (LoRA adapter) + same-effect (orthogonality preservation via manifold projection).

results.name: Calibrated Adaptation Bayesian Stiefel Manifold Priors PEFT (2602.17809)
results.description: Stiefel-Bayes Adapters with Matrix Langevin prior over orthonormal adapter factors, tangent-space Laplace approximation + geodesic retraction
results.rationale: Strong functional twin on Stiefel projection mechanism for orthogonal adapters with geodesic retraction (=rope-restore). The Bayesian Langevin prior gives a probabilistic tolerance around orthonormality which loosely parallels the sigma-tube concept (posterior spread ≈ relaxed orthogonality). Same artefact (orthogonal PEFT adapter) + same effect (controlled deviation from orthogonal manifold) by different mechanism (Bayesian prior vs explicit sigma scheduling).

results.name: Retraction-free optimization Stiefel manifold LoRA fine-tuning
results.description: Retraction-free penalty-free algorithm landing on Stiefel manifold for LoRA fine-tuning
results.rationale: Direct twin — addresses the same Stiefel-manifold landing problem for LoRA. Although 'retraction-free' is mechanistically OPPOSITE to the candidate's explicit Stiefel rope-restore (which is a retraction), the end-effect (parameters end up on Stiefel manifold for LoRA) is identical → v5 rubric 0.7+ (same artefact + same effect, different mechanism).

results.name: PoLAR Polar-Decomposed Low-Rank Adapter (2506.03133)
results.description: Polar-decomposed low-rank adapter representation enforcing orthogonal structure on LoRA factors
results.rationale: Polar decomposition is a different mechanism from Stiefel retraction/sigma-tube, but the effect (orthogonal-structure LoRA factor representation) is the same artefact + same effect as a sub-component of SLACK-TENSION-NULL (orthogonality on LoRA factors). Borderline 0.7 by v5 rubric.

results.name: OA-Adapter Budget-Adaptive Orthogonal Subspaces CL LLMs (2505.22358)
results.description: Dynamic budget adaptation + orthogonal subspace learning for continual learning
results.rationale: Strong twin — dynamic budget adaptation over orthogonal subspaces IS the adaptive sigma-tube-width scheduling concept (varying capacity/tolerance per task/layer). Same artefact (orthogonal-subspace adapter for LLMs) + same effect (adaptive capacity allocation for continual learning) by different mechanism (budget reallocation vs sigma-tube width).

results.name: BiLoRA Almost-Orthogonal Parameter Spaces CL CVPR 2025
results.description: Relaxed almost-orthogonal parameter subspaces for continual learning with adaptive tolerance vs strict orthogonality
results.rationale: EXACT TWIN on the headline novelty — 'almost-orthogonal parameter spaces with adaptive tolerance' IS the sigma-tube relaxed-orthogonality concept. Same artefact (LoRA-style orthogonal-subspace adapter) + same effect (relaxed orthogonality with adaptive width parameter) by very similar mechanism. Differs only in lacking explicit Stiefel retraction step.

results.name: CoSO Continuous Subspace Optimization Continual Learning (2505.11816)
results.description: Continuous adaptive subspace optimization across multiple subspaces — flexible rather than fixed
results.rationale: Continuous-subspace flexible adaptation parallels the adaptive sigma-tube concept. Same effect (adaptive subspace width/flexibility for continual learning) via continuous-optimization mechanism rather than sigma-tube + Stiefel retraction. Just at threshold by v5 rubric.

results.name: C-LoRA Continual Low-Rank Adaptation (2502.17920)
results.description: Learnable routing matrix dynamically managing parameter updates with orthogonality constraints
results.rationale: Same artefact (continual-learning LoRA with orthogonality constraints) but mechanism (learnable routing of updates) is quite different from sigma-tube width + Stiefel rope-restore. Just below 0.7 on functional but hits via keyword (LoRA + orthogonal) and semantic at threshold. Net hit via keyword overlap.

decision_rationale: All 8 retrieved results are hits under the disjunctive rule. Independent re-scoring confirms the primary verdict: SLACK-TENSION-NULL's core novel sub-components are each covered by prior art in the retrieval set — Stiefel-manifold LoRA retraction (2508.17901, 2602.17809, GP30inajOt), polar-orthogonal LoRA (2506.03133), adaptive-budget orthogonal subspaces (OA-Adapter 2505.22358), relaxed almost-orthogonal subspaces (BiLoRA CVPR2025) which is the headline twin for the sigma-tube concept, continuous adaptive subspaces (CoSO 2505.11816), and learnable orthogonality-constrained continual LoRA (C-LoRA 2502.17920). BiLoRA's 'almost-orthogonal parameter spaces with adaptive tolerance' is functionally equivalent to the sigma-tube width, and Stiefel-manifold LoRA papers cover the rope-restore projection. The candidate is a recombination of two well-established lines (Stiefel-LoRA + adaptive-orthogonal-CL) without a genuinely novel mechanism. FAIL.
